author: nero_eth
content:
In order to cap the nr of deposits/block on the EL, we can have a hard cap that is < 8192 (what the CL limit is today) and >728 (the max deposits per tx with 2**24 gas) to avoid running into DoS issues.

E.g. hard cap it at 1028, worst-case deposits of a tx are statically inferable: (tx.gas_limit - intrinsic_gas) // 23k (cost of a deposit). With EIP-7825's 16.78M tx gas cap that's at most ~728 deposits per tx. Since the cap (1024) is above that, every single tx is always includable.
More here:
https://github.com/nerolation/hegota-eip-presentations/blob/main/deposit_cap_eip.md

edit: realized this is essentially what [eip-8254](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8254) does, but adds the heuristic to prevent builder dos.

So eip-8254 + adopting the O(1) packing/building rule that protects against dos should be done in glam.
cc <@808969530608451584> <@412614104222531604>

author: jih2nn
content: Just had a convo with <@792404665068158998>. IIUC, the O(1) packing/building rule is stopping building when you hit the deposit limit. For instance, if you hit the deposit limit with, let's say, 15M gas left, you still stop including more transaction. I think that's the main difference with 8254.

author: nero_eth
content:
right yeah. 8254 could implicitly assume that builders figure that out themselves, but at the same time clients have to equip their local builder software with this dos protection as well.
Everything consensus-related stays as-is in 8254. The only thing that would change is mentioning the packing heuristic.

author: cperezz.eth
content:
<@547171287831805972>  Maria told me you were in charge of 8038 for EELS.

I have an implementation for reth already done 
(since I'm collecting data on it). 

I'd like to start contributing to all the test cases for 8038 if possible since I want to test my code ASAP

Woukd that be possible? Any reference branch or similar to kickstart the work?

author: potuz
content: I don't think there's any need for a cap for dos reasons? Deposits are not sig verified on place. The dos it's on builder deposits being on the same path as validator deposits. These can be handled by economic reasons, caches on clients or cleanly by moving builders deposits to a different contract (cc <@534934855113506836> ). I think for validator deposits, just having a limit on the CL that is higher than the EL and making sure that the hard payload size limit for broadcast is not hit, is enough

author: nero_eth
content:
yeah, I agree with all the points. I wasn't aware that validator deposits themselves have never been the problem but builder deposits. I agree, separate the paths + bump the ssz limit is the right path.
This would delay the fork tough. doing the ssz bump and instead cap deposits at something where we can handle builders + validators over the same deposit-path, dealing with invalid builder deposits and all the worst-cases.

author: nero_eth
content: We may need to split builder from validator deposit path anyway in this case. I now get that an EL cap won't help as the work is in `is_pending_validator`

author: tersec
content:
> economic reasons

This apparently did not convincingly cover all of the potential attacks.

It really is the case that either clients _must_ practically do a bunch of caching tricks or we split off the builders. No reference to cryptoeconomics avoids this

author: danceratopz
content:
Hi Carlos, I PR'd the spec and updated existing `./tests/amsterdam/` tests [here](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2972), it hasn't had review yet, feel free to review if you have time or simply base tests of that. Updating other test expectations was deferred until we have #2969 (more below).

I'm ooo today. Just copying the rough plan of action that I shared in our server to coordinate with <@830368514225340436>  yesterday. We're considering getting 2780 and 8038 in the same branch earlier than has been done with other EIPs to avoid conflicting gas limit expectations across the EIPs. Mario's #2969 should also help with that, so we planned to get that merged first!

> 1a. Top prio: As discussed in STEEL meeting review and merge [feat(test-specs): Allow automatic transaction gas-limit#2969](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2969).
> 
> 1b. Since it's already there, get [feat(spec-specs, tests): initial implementation of EIP-8038 state-access gas cost update](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2972) reviewed (at least specs and `./tests/amsterdam/` tests) and merge into `eips/amsterdam/eip-8038`.
> 
> 2a. Rebase `eips/amsterdam/eip-2780` on #2969.
> 
> 2b. Rebase `eips/amsterdam/eip-8038` on #2969.
> 
> 2c. TBD: Do we fix-up all the tests at this stage? 
> 
> 3a. Create `eips/amsterdam/eip-2780-8038` with8038 as the base (we can decide which EIP we use later, I believe you said it'd be more logical to merge 2780 into 8037)?
> 
> 4. Merge 2780 into `eips/amsterdam/eip-2780-8038` and fix-up any remaining test fails (if we deferred in 2c.) or new test fails.
